sourceMovie.title: Owning Mahowny
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Owning Mahowny for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Richard Kwietniowski keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jackie Brown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Owning Mahowny clicked for you, Jackie Brown (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Owning Mahowny?
faq.answer: Try Jackie Brown, The Town, Lucky Number Slevin—each shares DNA with Owning Mahowny while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Owning Mahowny?
faq.answer: Owning Mahowny (2003) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Owning Mahowny worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
